content: deep-improvement skill structure is self-referential on routing: INTINT_SIGNALS/RESOURCE_MAP/RUNTIME_ASSETS determine which sub-documents load
Three pluggable seams exist: candidate-source, dispatcher, scorer — Lane C can plug into the scorer seam for skill-benchmark mode
Lane-aware reference organization under references/ and assets/ — skill-benchmark harness must observe which refs/assets load vs should have loaded
sk-doc validates shape/package-structure, not behavioral usefulness — Lane C fills the behavioral gap
system-skill-advisor routes requests externally, does not measure internal skill router accuracy — Lane C operates one layer deeper
Coverage metric from Brown et al. 2024 (LLM Monkeys): coverage = skill-on task success minus skill-off success; scales log-linearly with sampling
Tau-Bench (Berkeley) establishes multi-round agent eval with tool-call trace capture — Lane C can use same trace capture for routing observation
Tool retrieval precision/recall (ToolACE, API-Bank, BMT): routing precision = right refs loaded, routing recall = all relevant refs loaded, argument binding ≈ INTENT_SIGNALS keyword match quality
LLM-as-judge for usefulness scoring with vs without skill context; requires careful prompt engineering to avoid circularity

Five candidate scoring dimensions: Routing/Activation Accuracy, Discovery Precision+Recall, Efficiency/Bottlenecks, Usefulness (skill-on vs skill-off ablation), Structural Connectivity (orphan refs, dead router keys)
Normalization across skill shapes: fractional scores per skill size, per-dimension z-scores against population, complexity-weighted aggregation
loop-host.cjs --mode=skill-benchmark is the correct entry point following Lane B pattern; dispatcher remains skill-advisor, scorer becomes Lane C benchmark scorer
